articles.title: Software Is Made Between Commits
articles.summary: Software is made between commits, which are discrete snapshots of a working version of the code. This approach was not designed for collaboration with agents, but rather for human-to-human communication in the same worktree. The problems associated with this method become more significant when collaborating with agents, as the conversation that generates the code becomes the true source of the software. DeltaDB is a new kind of version control built on a single coherent abstraction that transforms conversations into shared artifacts and enables collaboration with agents.

articles.title: Reuse Less Software
articles.summary: The problem is that supply chain attacks are becoming increasingly common due to a cost model shift from sharing and distributing software to automating its creation and maintenance at a lower cost. This has led to the proliferation of cheap, automated software that can be easily broken or compromised. The solution proposed in this article involves leveraging existing technologies such as modularity, structured programming, and package repositories like CPAN and Linux distros to build complex software systems without relying on vendors for integration work.

The bill grants broad authority to compel electronic service providers to implement interception capabilities, creating cybersecurity vulnerabilities that could be exploited by criminals and hostile foreign actors. This move also undermines the government's stated commitment to protecting Canadians against unreasonable search and seizure.

articles.title: CVE-2026-45257: LPE in FreeBSD via kTLS-RX
articles.summary: A significant kernel bug has been discovered in FreeBSD that allows an unprivileged user on a default version of the operating system to write attacker-influenced bytes into any file they have read access to. This vulnerability, dubbed BUMSRAKETE, has a severity rating of 13/10 and is considered one of the biggest kernel bugs ever found. The bug resides in the unsafe composition of three FreeBSD subsystems that are individually correct, making it difficult to detect. It allows for a significant increase in file-writable access, potentially leading to high-level security risks such as root shell crashes and compromised confidentiality.

articles.title: Artemis III's life support: How a spacesuit keeps astronauts alive on the moon
articles.summary: Astronauts on NASA's Artemis III and IV missions will wear a new life support system designed by Axiom Space, featuring an inner suit called the Liquid Cooling and Ventilation Garment (LCVG) that is more closely resembling a sleek superhero outfit than past versions. The LCVG circulates cold water across major muscle groups to absorb metabolic heat and pump it out through oversized backpacks like the portable life support system. This advanced technology, courtesy of Italian fashion house Prada, aims to keep astronauts alive on the moon's surface by maintaining safe pressure and preventing organ failure due to heatstroke and physical exertion.
